dialogues: Look at this rectangle. You know it shows 4 times 5 equals 20. But there's more than one way to write that fact.
That's right. Both of those describe the same rectangle. Two ways to write the same fact.
Now finish this sentence about why both equations work.
Exactly. Two ways to write the same fact. The equal sign lets you write multiplication either way.
That's it. Both sides have the same value. That's what the equal sign tells you.
